chapters.title: The Last Lesson by Alphonse Daudet
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Loss of language equals loss of identity. The chapter highlights patriotism, regret, and the importance of mother tongue.

chapters.title: Lost Spring by Anees Jung
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Child labour, poverty, exploitation, and the vicious cycle of tradition that kills dreams.

chapters.title: Deep Water by William Douglas
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Conquering fear through determination, willpower, and systematic effort. The lesson contrasts fear of death with fear itself.

chapters.title: The Rattrap by Selma Lagerlöf
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Human goodness can reform a person. Compassion and dignity bring out the best in people.

chapters.title: Indigo by Louis Fischer
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: First instance of Satyagraha in India. The chapter highlights self-reliance, courage, non-violence, and leadership that empowers the poor.

chapters.title: Poets and Pancakes by Asokamitran
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Humor and irony in the film world. The chapter shows the gap between art and commercial cinema and between bureaucracy and creativity.

chapters.title: The Interview by Christopher Silvester / Umberto Eco
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: The chapter explores the value and danger of interviews, the ethics of public questioning, and Umberto Eco’s discipline in using small moments of time.

chapters.title: Going Places by A. R. Barton
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Fantasy versus reality, adolescent dreams, class barriers, and escapism.

chapters.title: My Mother at Sixty-six by Kamala Das
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Ageing, separation, fear of loss, and the bond between mother and daughter. The poem shows how we hide pain behind smiles when facing mortality.

chapters.title: Keeping Quiet by Pablo Neruda
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Need for introspection, peace, and universal brotherhood. Silence helps us understand ourselves and stop harming nature and each other.

chapters.title: A Thing of Beauty by John Keats
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Beauty is eternal and gives us hope. In a world full of pain and evil, beauty in nature and art heals us and gives meaning to life.

chapters.title: A Roadside Stand by Robert Frost
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Economic inequality, exploitation of the rural poor, false promises of government, and the city-village divide.

chapters.title: Aunt Jennifer's Tigers by Adrienne Rich
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Male domination in marriage, suppression of women, and art as an escape. The tigers show Aunt Jennifer’s inner desire for freedom.

chapters.title: The Third Level by Jack Finney
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Escapism, nostalgia for a simpler past, and the harshness of modern life. The third level is a metaphor for the human desire to escape stress and insecurity.

chapters.title: The Tiger King by Kalki
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Abuse of power, pride, fate, and satire on rulers who misuse power for vanity. The story also suggests that harming nature backfires.

chapters.title: Journey to the End of the Earth by Tishani Doshi
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Climate change, environmental responsibility, Earth’s history, and the need for global cooperation.

chapters.title: The Enemy by Pearl S. Buck
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Conflict between human compassion and national loyalty, duty as a doctor versus duty as a citizen, and humanity rising above hatred.

chapters.title: On the Face of It by Susan Hill
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Disability, loneliness, self-acceptance, empathy, and the importance of inner worth over outward appearance.

chapters.title: Memories of Childhood by Zitkala-Sa and Bama
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Racial discrimination, caste discrimination, cultural suppression, humiliation, resistance, and the power of education.
